title_en: Introduction and the Beginning of Creation
content_en:
Al-Kamil fi al-Tarikh (The Complete History) by Izz al-Din Ibn al-Athir (555–630 AH / 1160–1233 CE) stands as one of the most comprehensive universal histories ever produced in the Islamic tradition. Ibn al-Athir, a scholar from Jazira (northern Mesopotamia), devoted decades to compiling a chronicle that begins with the creation of the world and extends to his own era in the early thirteenth century.

The work opens with a theological and cosmological introduction. Ibn al-Athir situates history within an Islamic worldview: all events unfold according to divine providence, and the purpose of studying the past is to draw lessons (ibra) that guide present conduct. He cites Quranic verses that command reflection on the fate of earlier nations and emphasizes that history is not mere storytelling but a form of wisdom.

The Creation of the Heavens and Earth

Following classical Islamic cosmography, Ibn al-Athir describes the creation of the heavens and earth over six days, drawing on Quranic accounts supplemented by narrations transmitted from the Companions and their successors. He records differing scholarly opinions on the nature of the primordial waters, the creation of light, and the order in which celestial bodies were fashioned, presenting them without sectarian preference.

He then addresses the creation of the angels and their roles as intermediaries between the divine will and the created order. The account of Iblis (Satan) and his refusal to prostrate before Adam is narrated with particular care, as Ibn al-Athir uses it to introduce the theme of human moral agency that will recur throughout the chronicle.

The Creation of Adam and the Early Prophets

The narrative of Adam's creation, his dwelling in Paradise, and his descent to earth sets the stage for the long prophetic history that occupies much of the early volumes. Ibn al-Athir follows the conventions of Islamic universal history established by al-Tabari and al-Masudi, treating the biblical patriarchs and Quranic prophets as the first phase of providential history.

He records the story of Habil (Abel) and Qabil (Cain) as the first human tragedy, and proceeds through the generations of Adam's descendants, noting the length of their lifespans as recorded in Islamic tradition. This section reflects Ibn al-Athir's method of collating reports from multiple chains of transmission and presenting them in a coherent narrative while acknowledging variant accounts.

Methodology and Sources

Ibn al-Athir's methodological preface explains his reliance on earlier chronicles, particularly the monumental history of al-Tabari, which he frequently condenses and refines. He also draws on Ibn al-Kalbi, al-Masudi, al-Waqidi, and Ibn Ishaq for specific periods. His contribution lies not only in synthesis but in critical selection: he omits weak and implausible reports that cluttered earlier works, aiming for a history that is both reliable and readable. This opening section establishes the intellectual confidence and organizational clarity that characterize the entire work.
content_id:
Al-Kamil fi al-Tarikh (Sejarah yang Lengkap) karya Izz al-Din Ibn al-Athir (555–630 H / 1160–1233 M) merupakan salah satu sejarah universal paling komprehensif yang pernah dihasilkan dalam tradisi Islam. Ibn al-Athir, seorang ulama dari Jazira (Mesopotamia utara), mendedikasikan puluhan tahun untuk menyusun sebuah kronik yang mencakup sejarah dari penciptaan hingga zamannya sendiri, mencakup seluruh dinasti, penaklukan, bencana, dan perkembangan intelektual yang membentuk dunia Islam.

Karya ini terdiri dari dua belas volume besar yang dimulai dengan kisah penciptaan: Arsy, Kursi, langit, bumi, dan makhluk hidup. Ibn al-Athir mengikuti tradisi historiografi Islam dalam memulai narasi sejarah dengan unsur-unsur kosmologis sebelum beralih ke kisah para nabi dan umat manusia. Ia menggabungkan tafsir Al-Qur'an, hadis Nabi, dan tradisi isra'iliyyat dengan cermat, senantiasa membedakan antara riwayat yang sahih dan yang lemah.

Metodologi Ibn al-Athir menonjol karena pendekatannya yang analitis. Berbeda dengan para sejarawan sebelumnya yang cenderung menyusun sejarah dalam format annalistik (tahun demi tahun) tanpa banyak analisis, Ibn al-Athir berusaha memahami sebab-akibat peristiwa sejarah. Beliau juga dikenal karena objektivitasnya dalam mencatat kemenangan maupun kekalahan umat Islam, serta dalam memberikan penilaian yang berimbang terhadap tokoh-tokoh kontroversial.

Dalam bagian pembuka ini, Ibn al-Athir membahas penciptaan alam semesta berdasarkan sumber-sumber Al-Qur'an dan hadis, kemudian beralih ke kisah Adam (as) sebagai manusia pertama, penciptaan Hawa, dan ujian pertama yang dihadapi manusia di Taman Eden. Kisah ini bukan sekadar latar belakang kosmologis, melainkan fondasi teologis bagi seluruh narasi sejarah yang menyusul.

Al-Kamil fi al-Tarikh menjadi rujukan utama bagi generasi sejarawan berikutnya dan masih dipelajari hingga kini sebagai sumber primer yang tak ternilai untuk memahami sejarah Islam klasik.
